Just now

--

Keep private prompts on a local model, send the hard ones to the cloud, and run the same code across a laptop, a workstation, and an Azure data center.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

One Stack, Two Tiers: Hybrid AI Routing on AMD ROCm From Ryzen AI to AzureThis article walks through a practical way to run AI across two tiers of compute at once: a local model on an AMD Ryzen AI machine and a frontier model in the cloud, unified by a single open software stack, AMD ROCm.
You will see how the pieces fit together end to end, an OpenAI-compatible endpoint at the front, a semantic router, a local inference server for work that should stay private or cheap, and an Azure-hosted model for requests that need heavier reasoning.
By the end, you will understand the architecture, the job each component does, and how the same model can move from a laptop to an Azure Instinct GPU with few or no code changes.
Reference here.
Why route at all: the local versus frontier tradeoff
Most AI applications are built around a single decision made too early. You either wire everything to a frontier model in the cloud and accept the rate limits, the API throttling, and the per-token cost, or you run everything locally and give up on the hard reasoning tasks.
